2606.15037.title: ReportQA: QA-Based Radiology Report Evaluation
2606.15037.paper_summary: Radiology report evaluation is essential for advancing automated report generation. Natural language generation metrics have limited clinical relevance. Clinical efficacy (CE) metrics evaluate important medical findings, but focus mainly on presence and cover only a limited set of entities. Due to heavy reliance on manual annotations, it is difficult for CE metrics to extend clinical entities or attributes. In clinical practice, radiology reports serve as a medium for information transfer. Clinicians use them to perform downstream diagnostic tasks without directly inspecting images. Based on this insight, we propose ReportQA, a clinical-related and flexible radiology report evaluation framework, supporting detailed quantitative analysis of radiology report generation systems. We first collect datasets covering multiple imaging modalities and anatomical regions. We then construct knowledge trees of clinical entities and attributes with radiologist guidance, and use large language models (LLMs) to extract structured information from raw reports. Next, we generate QA pairs from predefined templates and apply quality control through self-filtering and report-based filtering. During evaluation, the report is treated as context, and an LLM acts as a judge model to answer the QA pairs. Based on the resulting QA accuracy, we introduce QAScore metric. Compared with existing metrics, QAScore shows better alignment with radiologist judgments. Experiments on multiple state-of-the-art vision-language models reveal that current report-based inference paradigms struggle to learn fine-grained clinical representations and exhibit strong negative prior biases. In contrast, question-driven inference provides a more effective alternative. For reproducibility and extensibility, we release the knowledge trees, structured reports, and QA pairs, along with the pipeline code for QA construction and evaluation.
2606.15037.paper_summary_zh: 放射學報告評估對於推進自動報告生成至關重要。自然語言生成指標在臨床上的相關性有限。臨床有效性（CE）指標評估重要的醫療發現，但主要集中於存在性，且僅涵蓋有限的實體集合。由於對手動註釋的高度依賴，CE 指標難以擴展臨床實體或屬性。在臨床實踐中，放射學報告作為信息傳遞的媒介。臨床醫生利用它們執行下游診斷任務，而無需直接檢查影像。基於這一見解，我們提出了 ReportQA，一個與臨床相關且靈活的放射學報告評估框架，支持對放射學報告生成系統的詳細定量分析。我們首先收集涵蓋多種影像模態和解剖區域的數據集。然後，在放射科醫生的指導下，我們構建臨床實體和屬性的知識樹，並使用大型語言模型（LLMs）從原始報告中提取結構化信息。接下來，我們從預定義模板生成 QA 對，並通過自我過濾和基於報告的過濾應用質量控制。在評估過程中，報告被視為上下文，LLM 作為判斷模型回答 QA 對。根據生成的 QA 準確性，我們引入 QAScore 指標。與現有指標相比，QAScore 與放射科醫生的判斷顯示出更好的對齊。對多個最先進的視覺-語言模型的實驗顯示，當前基於報告的推理範式難以學習細緻的臨床表徵，並表現出強烈的負先驗偏見。相比之下，基於問題的推理提供了更有效的替代方案。為了可重複性和可擴展性，我們釋放了知識樹、結構化報告和 QA 對，以及用於 QA 構建和評估的管道代碼。
2606.15037.author: Yiming Shi et.al.
2606.15037.authors: Yiming Shi, Shaoshuai Yang, Xi Chen, Haolin Li, Hengyu Zhang, Che Jiang, Kaiwen Wang, Xun Zhu, Dong Xie, Fei Wang, Dejing Dou, Miao Li, Ji Wu
